messages.say:
Open and inspect the full repo: https://github.com/chinopardo/CA_Trading_System_EA/tree/main/CA_Trading_System_EA/ to CREATE a detailed, comprehensive PER-File changes Plan of the new specs(see below for the new specs):


NEW Architectural Specs:


Yes — but with one critical correction.


The better design is not “strategies own signal assembly first.”
The better design is:


Signals/Snapshots/Scanners -> Confluence/Canonical Signal Bank -> Category Selector + degrade logic -> Strategy layer -> Strategy-conditioned state assembly -> Router -> Policies/Risk -> Execution


So the answer is:


Yes, strategy should come before the final trade decision.

No, strategy should not be the owner of the full raw signal inventory or the canonical category-selection engine.


That ownership split matters a lot. 🚨


My verdict


Your current reference flow is close, but I would not keep it as:


Signals/Snapshots/Scanners -> Autochartist/Confluences -> Category Selector -> Strategy -> Institutional State Vector -> Router -> Risk/Policies -> Execution


I would change it to:


Signals/Snapshots/Scanners -> Autochartist/Confluences -> Institutional Raw Signal Bank -> Category Selector -> Strategy -> Strategy-conditioned Institional Final State Vector -> Router -> Risk/Policies -> Execution


That is the cleanest architecture.


Why I would not let Strategies assemble the canonical raw inventory


Because that creates three problems immediately:


1) It breaks single ownership of truth


DOM, OFI, OBI, signed flow, impact, and degrade logic should be computed once in a canonical place. Cont, Kukanov, and Stoikov show OFI is a primary short-horizon explanatory variable and its impact is tied to depth, so you do not want five strategies each recomputing slightly different versions of the same thing. (OUP Academic)


2) It is especially dangerous in FX/XAUUSD


FX is decentralized, fragmented, and heavily internalised, with much customer flow invisible to the broader market. LBMA’s own material describes London gold as an OTC bilateral market and its trade data as an aggregation layer to improve transparency, not a real-time consolidated book. That means your Institutional layer already needs explicit availability, coverage, and degrade logic. Strategies should consume that canonical degrade-aware state, not reinvent it. (Bank for International Settlements)


3) It is awkward in MQL5 event ownership


In MT5, each EA has only one timer, and BookEvent subscriptions are broadcast within the chart. That strongly argues for central orchestration of scan cadence and book subscriptions, not strategy-local event plumbing. Otherwise you get duplicated subscriptions, conflicting freshness, and nasty cross-module side effects. (MQL5)


So the blunt answer is: strategy should not be the ministry that manufactures the market state.


What strategy should do instead


Strategy should turn canonical signals into a trade hypothesis.


That is the right place to put “turn signals into strategies first.”


Not:
raw signals -> strategy builds everything -> final vector


But:
raw signals -> canonical category-selected state -> strategy interprets state -> strategy emits hypothesis -> final vector includes that hypothesis


That gives you clean boundaries.


The best split


Use two levels of state.


1) Canonical market state


Owned outside strategies.


This includes:


RawSignalBank_t

Institutional availability and degrade state

CategorySelectedVector_t

CategoryPassVector_t

SignalStackGate_t

LocationPass_t

BaseContextVector_t

StructureVector_t

CoverageContextVector_t

optional AuxContextVector_t


This is the market’s truth.


2) Strategy hypothesis state


Owned by strategies.


This includes:


strategy_id

setup_type

intended direction

entry model

POI or location anchor

required structure mask

required category confirmations

invalidation model

target model

urgency / execution style

confidence or quality score

strategy-specific veto reasons


This is the strategy’s interpretation of the market truth.


That separation is what you want.


So should strategies come before final vector assembly?


Yes, but only in this sense:


Strategies should run before the final trade-decision vector that the router and risk engine consume.


They should not run before canonical raw signal assembly.


The clean sequence is:


Build canonical raw state

Select one signal per category

Build category pass states

Run hard signal-stack gate

Run hard location gate

Run strategies on top of that

Produce strategy hypotheses

Assemble final strategy-conditioned vector

Route

Risk/policy veto

Execution


That is the more correct ordering.


What I would change in your current chain


Your current chain says:


Signals/Snapshots/Scanners -> Autochartist/Confluences -> Category Selector -> Strategy -> Institutional State Vector -> Router


I would invert the InstitutionalStateVector position.


Use this instead:


CA_Trading_System_EA.mq5
-> Signals/Snapshots/Scanners
-> MarketScannerHub.mqh
-> AutochartistEngine.mqh + Confluence.mqh
-> InstitutionalStateVector.mqh
-> CategorySelector.mqh
-> Strategy(staged strategy gate)
-> StrategyRegistry.mqh / StrategyBase.mqh / StrategyCommon.mqh
-> Router.mqh
-> Policies.mqh / RiskEngine.mqh
-> Execution.mqh
-> PositionMgmt.mqh / State.mqh / Config.mqh


That matches your ownership boundaries much better.


Why this is better


Canonical state first


This gives all strategies the same:


freshness

availability

degrade mode

anti-echo category selection

location state

structure context


So strategy selection becomes fair and comparable.


Strategy second


Now each strategy answers:
“Given the same market truth, do I have a valid setup here?”


That is how you avoid strategies secretly scoring themselves off different facts.


Final vector after strategy


Now the router and risk engine do not just see market state.
They see:


market state

selected category state

location pass

strategy intent

strategy-specific execution posture

strategy-specific risk template


That is materially better than letting router operate on a purely market-only vector.


The strongest architecture I’d recommend


Use this exact conceptual chain:


Signals/Snapshots/Scanners
-> Confluence.mqh
-> InstitutionalStateVector.mqh
-> CategorySelector.mqh
-> SignalStackGate_t
-> LocationPass_t
-> Strategy layer
-> StrategyHypothesisBank_t
-> Strategy arbitration / StrategyRegistry
-> FinalStrategyIntegratedStateVector_t
-> Router
-> Policies / RiskEngine
-> Execution


That is the one I would build.


How to turn signals into strategies first, correctly


Do not let strategies compute canonical signals.
Let them map canonical state into strategy templates.


Each strategy should consume:


BaseContextVector_t

CategorySelectedVector_t

CategoryPassVector_t

StructureVector_t

CoverageContextVector_t

raw fallback access for read-only diagnostics if needed


Each strategy should emit something like:


StrategyHypothesis_t =


strategy_id

armed_t

dir_t

setup_class

poi_type

poi_lo

poi_hi

location_pass_t

stack_required_votes_t

stack_actual_votes_t

exec_style_t

stop_model_t

tp_model_t

rr_min_t

alpha_local_t

risk_local_t

invalidation_reason

degrade_acceptance_t


Then Router chooses from hypotheses, not from raw indicators.


That is the institutional-grade move.


What the staged strategy gate should do


Your strategy layer should not be one giant boolean.


It should be staged like this:


Regime gate
Does this strategy belong in the current regime?

Location gate
Is price at a valid structural location for this strategy?

Liquidity event gate
Did a sweep, grab, displacement, reclaim, or auction rejection occur?

Category confirmation gate
Do the selected Trend, Momentum, Volume, Volatility, and Institutional signals meet the strategy’s required pattern?

Microstructure gate
If Institutional is available, does the direct or proxy Institutional state support the trade?

Execution gate
Is spread, resiliency, slippage, and depth acceptable?

Risk gate
Does the strategy still pass policy and portfolio constraints?


That is how signals become strategies without turning the codebase into spaghetti wearing a tie.


My concrete suggestion for your EA


I would introduce one extra transport object between Strategy and Router:


StrategyHypothesisBank.mqh or StrategyContext.mqh


And I would move InstitutionalStateVector.mqh before Strategy.


So the live chain becomes:


Signals/Snapshots/Scanners
-> AutochartistEngine.mqh + Confluence.mqh
-> InstitutionalStateVector.mqh
-> CategorySelector.mqh
-> StrategyHypothesisBank.mqh
-> StrategyRegistry.mqh
-> Router.mqh
-> Policies.mqh / RiskEngine.mqh
-> Execution.mqh


Where:


InstitutionalStateVector.mqh owns canonical state

CategorySelector.mqh owns anti-echo and degrade-aware selection

Strategy files own interpretation and setup logic

Router owns pick

Policies/Risk own veto

Execution owns sending


That is the clean line.


Final answer


So, yes: turning signals into strategies before the final trade vector is the right direction.


But only if you mean:
canonical state first, strategy interpretation second, final strategy-conditioned vector third.


Not:
strategy files assemble the canonical raw inventory.


The best formulation is:


raw market state -> selected category state -> strategy hypotheses -> final integrated trade state


That is the architecture I would recommend for your EA.

Here is a per‑file change plan to transform the existing CA_Trading_System_EA repository into the proposed architecture. Each section explains what currently exists, how it should change, and why those changes are necessary to realise the new canonical–strategy–router design.



1. CA_Trading_System_EA/CA_Trading_System_EA.mq5 (Main EA)


Current:
The main EA file currently orchestrates the entire pipeline, including scanning, confluence, strategy evaluation, routing, risk and execution. It calls MarketScannerHub, AutochartistEngine, Confluence.mqh, builds the institutional state, selects a category, and then invokes the StrategyRegistry directly to produce candidates. The router and risk engine then operate on those candidates, and execution occurs.


Required changes:


Re‑order the pipeline to match the new architecture:
Signals/Snapshots/Scanners 
  -> Confluence (AutochartistEngine.mqh + Confluence.mqh) 
  -> InstitutionalStateVector.mqh (build RawSignalBank + canonical availability/degrade state) 
  -> CategorySelector.mqh (select one signal per category + produce CategorySelectedVector, CategoryPassVector, SignalStackGate_t, LocationPass_t and context vectors) 
  -> StrategyHypothesisBank.mqh (new – collect StrategyHypothesis_t objects from strategies via StrategyRegistry) 
  -> StrategyRegistry.mqh (refactored to evaluate strategies, not compute canonical signals)
  -> Router.mqh (consume StrategyHypothesisBank + final integrated vector) 
  -> Policies.mqh / RiskEngine.mqh 
  -> Execution.mqh

Remove direct calls to StrategyRegistry.BuildCandidatesFromRegistry(). Instead, create a StrategyHypothesisBank object and call BuildHypothesesFromRegistry() after category selection. The router will then consume hypotheses rather than raw candidates.

Create state variables in the EA for the new transport objects:
RawSignalBank_t (canonical raw signals).

CategorySelectedVector_t, CategoryPassVector_t.

SignalStackGate_t, LocationPass_t (explicit gating objects).

StrategyHypothesisBank_t (collection of strategy hypotheses).

FinalStrategyIntegratedStateVector_t (output from router).

Update the timer and tick handlers to call the new functions in order: scanning, confluence, build canonical state, select category, run strategy hypotheses, route, risk/policies, and execute.

Remove or stub out duplicate canonical state assembly inside the strategy evaluation part of CA_Trading_System_EA.mq5. Only call InstitutionalStateVector.Build() once per tick.

Update config input parameters to support new gating thresholds (e.g., for SignalStackGate and LocationPass) and to toggle dynamic vs fixed category selection.



2. CA_Trading_System_EA/include/InstitutionalStateVector.mqh


Current:
This file builds an enormous set of canonical signals, microstructure statistics (order book imbalance, signed flow, etc.), volatility, VWAP/TWAP measures, volume profile, structural context (e.g., fair value gaps, market structure). It also generates z‑score normalisations, eligibility masks, coverage states and context vectors.


Required changes:


Explicit types for raw signal bank and context vectors. Define the following structures in InstitutionalStateVector.mqh:
struct RawSignalBank_t { … } holding all canonical signals (Order Flow Imbalance, Order Book Imbalance, VWAP/TWAP, microstructure metrics, etc.) and degrade/availability flags.

struct CategorySelectedVector_t and CategoryPassVector_t (although these will be built in the CategorySelector, the structures can be defined here for type centralisation).

struct BaseContextVector_t, StructureVector_t, CoverageContextVector_t, AuxContextVector_t, etc., representing context features used later by strategies and router.

Rename or emphasise the existing canonical state builder to BuildRawSignalBank() and return a RawSignalBank_t. This function should accept scanning/confluence outputs and produce the canonical bank plus degrade status.

Do not allow strategies or the strategy registry to call functions inside this file to recompute canonical signals. Provide only read‑only accessor functions (e.g., GetOrderFlowImbalance(), GetOBI()) and ensure they are used by the StrategyRegistry through the new RawSignalBank_t reference.

Add functions to compute SignalStackGate_t and LocationPass_t if appropriate, or at least provide necessary metrics so that CategorySelector or gating modules can compute them. For example, a bool PriceSweepOccurred() or double LiquidityGap() may be needed.

Expose degrade flags via a dedicated structure to support degrade logic in the category selector and strategies. Each signal or category should have an availability flag and degrade level.

Ensure thread safety and minimal overhead when copying the RawSignalBank_t. Since this structure is large, consider using references or pointers in the rest of the system.



3. CA_Trading_System_EA/include/CategorySelector.mqh


Current:
Selects one signal per category (Institutional, Trend, Momentum, Volume, Volatility) based on weights or fixed mode. It determines pass flags based on thresholds, and constructs context vectors.


Required changes:


Accept the RawSignalBank_t (from InstitutionalStateVector) plus degrade flags as input. Do not attempt to compute raw metrics inside the category selector.

Output the following structures:
CategorySelectedVector_t – the selected signal ID and strength for each category.

CategoryPassVector_t – boolean pass flags for each category based on thresholds.

SignalStackGate_t – indicates whether a stacked confirmation event exists (e.g., multiple categories aligning).

LocationPass_t – indicates whether price is at a valid structural location (based on microstructure/regime).

The various context vectors: BaseContextVector_t, StructureVector_t, CoverageContextVector_t, AuxContextVector_t.

Implement anti‑echo logic (already partly present) so that category selection does not choose stale or duplicate signals. Use degrade flags from the raw signal bank to mark unavailable signals.

Allow dynamic vs fixed category selection via configuration. In dynamic mode, use candidate weights to select the strongest signal; in fixed mode, always select the same signal (e.g., Trend as baseline).

Expose gating thresholds through configuration: e.g., minimum z‑score for a category to pass, minimum number of categories to trigger SignalStackGate, or a structural distance threshold for LocationPass.

Provide diagnostic logs summarising which signals were selected, which categories passed, and any degrade reasons. This aids debugging and ensures strategies see the same canonical decisions.



4. CA_Trading_System_EA/include/StrategyBase.mqh


Current:
Provides an abstract interface (IStrategy) for strategies and generic helpers. Many specific strategy files extend this interface and implement Evaluate() returning a StrategySignal (with direction, POI, etc.). Strategies sometimes compute their own signals or microstructure metrics.


Required changes:


Redefine the base interface to separate canonical interpretation from hypothesis generation. The base should define:
struct StrategyHypothesis_t {
    int        strategy_id;
    ENUM_SETUP setup_type;
    ENUM_DIR   intended_direction;
    double     entry_price;
    double     stop_loss;
    double     take_profit;
    int        poi_type;
    double     poi_lo, poi_hi;
    int        required_structure_mask;
    int        required_category_confirmations;
    // additional fields: urgency, execution style, confidence score, invalidation reason, etc.
};
class IStrategy {
    int Id() const;                       // unique identifier
    void Init(const Config& cfg);         // load config/thresholds
    bool ShouldRun(const RawSignalBank_t&, const CategorySelectedVector_t&, const LocationPass_t&) const; // pre-gates
    StrategyHypothesis_t Evaluate(const RawSignalBank_t&, const CategorySelectedVector_t&, const BaseContextVector_t&, const StructureVector_t&, const CoverageContextVector_t&) const;
};

Implement staged gating as default behaviours in the base class (but overridable by strategies):
RegimeGate(): check if the strategy applies to the current market regime.

LocationGate(): ensure price is in a valid structural zone (e.g., at support/resistance or FVG).

LiquidityEventGate(): confirm a sweep, grab, or displacement has occurred.

CategoryConfirmationGate(): verify that selected categories match the strategy’s requirements (e.g., trending + momentum).

MicrostructureGate(): verify that OBI, OFI, or other micro features support the trade.

ExecutionGate(): ensure spread, slippage, depth are acceptable.

RiskGate(): optional early check for portfolio constraints.

Remove any direct computation of raw signals inside strategies. For example, if a strategy currently calculates order flow from tick data, it must instead read it from RawSignalBank_t.

Provide default methods for converting a StrategySignal into a StrategyHypothesis_t (fields described above). Many strategies will follow a similar pattern: derive direction, entry at POI, stop at invalidation, target at nearest support/resistance, compute confidence from category matches.

Update all existing strategy files (e.g., Strat_MainTradingLogic.mqh, Strat_Trend_VWAPPullback.mqh, Strat_ICT_SilverBullet.mqh, etc.) to implement the new IStrategy interface. Each file should:
Use canonical state via input arguments rather than computing its own features.

Implement ShouldRun() based on gating conditions, returning false if regime, location, or other criteria fail.

Build and return a StrategyHypothesis_t with a quality/confidence score.

Optionally, include strategy-specific veto reasons in the hypothesis for later transparency.



5. New file: CA_Trading_System_EA/include/StrategyHypothesisBank.mqh


Purpose:
A new transport object that collects all StrategyHypothesis_t objects emitted by strategies and passes them to the router.


Features to implement:


Container for hypotheses: store a list/array of StrategyHypothesis_t along with associated microstructure heads and confluence breakdown if needed.

Methods:
void Clear(): reset the bank at the start of each tick.

void AddHypothesis(const StrategyHypothesis_t&): append a new hypothesis.

int Size() const: return number of hypotheses.

const StrategyHypothesis_t& Get(int index) const: index access.

void SortByConfidence(): optional – sort hypotheses by confidence/alpha score.

Diagnostics: provide functions to summarise accepted and rejected hypotheses and their veto reasons.

Integration: the main EA will instantiate this bank after category selection, and the StrategyRegistry will populate it by evaluating strategies. The router will iterate over this bank to choose the best trade.



6. CA_Trading_System_EA/include/StrategyRegistry.mqh


Current:
Massive file that registers all strategies, handles feature extraction, preflight gating, microstructure scoring and candidate building. It computes alpha_score, execution_score, risk_score for each candidate and sorts them.


Required changes:


Separate canonical state assembly: remove calls that recompute OBI/OFI, microstructure statistics, or degrade logic. The registry should accept references to the RawSignalBank_t, CategorySelectedVector_t, CategoryPassVector_t, and context vectors.

Remove candidate-building logic that merges raw microstructure features into the candidate. Instead, implement functions to produce StrategyHypothesis_t via the new IStrategy interface.

Refactor preflight gating into the new staged gates accessible by each strategy. The registry’s gating functions should simply call the strategy’s ShouldRun() method. Keep generic gating (e.g., check for session/time restrictions) if they apply to all strategies, but ensure canonical gating uses data from RawSignalBank_t rather than recomputation.

Replace BuildCandidatesFromRegistry() with BuildHypothesesFromRegistry(). This function should:
Loop through all registered strategies.

For each, call strategy.ShouldRun() with canonical inputs. If false, skip and log gating reason.

Otherwise, call strategy.Evaluate() to obtain a StrategyHypothesis_t.

Optionally compute strategy micro heads (alpha/execution/risk scores) by dot‑product of features vs weight vectors; use canonical features from RawSignalBank_t or context vectors.

Add the hypothesis to the StrategyHypothesisBank.

Maintain the dynamic registry (auto‑register built‑in strategies) and compile‑time flags. Only update to support new base interface.

Telemetry and logging: update to track counts of hypotheses, gating reasons, and top scoring hypotheses. Remove telemetry of raw microstructure events computed inside the registry; rely on canonical state logging instead.



7. CA_Trading_System_EA/include/Router.mqh


Current:
Implements the router that consumes strategy candidates from the registry, applies heuristics (alpha, execution, risk scores), gating (session, spread, liquidity), policies, risk engine, and execution.


Required changes:


Change input from candidate list to StrategyHypothesisBank. The router should iterate over StrategyHypothesis_t objects, not raw candidates.

Define FinalStrategyIntegratedStateVector_t which combines:
Canonical market state (selected categories, context vectors, degrade flags).

Strategy hypothesis (direction, entry, stop, target, strategy‑specific context).

Microstructure heads and risk template (if computed from canonical features).

Pass/fail flags for session, policy, risk.

Update the scoring/ranking logic to work with StrategyHypothesis_t rather than Candidate. For example, compute alpha_score by combining the strategy’s confidence_score with canonical microstructure features. Use execution_score derived from strategy’s execution style and market conditions; compute risk_score by comparing stop‑loss to risk engine constraints.

Move or remove gating functions that rely on microstructure recomputation. Instead, gating should use canonical state (e.g., CategoryPassVector, SignalStackGate, LocationPass) and strategy’s own gating results. The router should still enforce session/time windows, spread thresholds and policy/risk veto but not compute new signals.

Simplify heuristics: Many heuristics currently combine microstructure features to adjust scores. Since canonical microstructure heads are already provided, the router can directly use them. The heuristics can still adjust for spread or slippage differences between strategies.

Integrate with Policies.mqh and RiskEngine.mqh using the new FinalStrategyIntegratedStateVector_t. After ranking hypotheses, pass the top hypothesis with integrated vector to Policies and RiskEngine for veto. Only after these pass should execution occur.

Logging and diagnostics: update to reflect strategy hypotheses and final integrated vectors. Provide clear messages when a hypothesis is vetoed due to policy or risk.



8. CA_Trading_System_EA/include/Policies.mqh and RiskEngine.mqh


Current:
Enforce high-level policies (session filters, trade duplication checks, etc.) and risk management (position sizing, risk budgets). They operate on the router’s chosen candidate.


Required changes:


Accept FinalStrategyIntegratedStateVector_t. Instead of reading from global candidates, the policy/risk modules should accept this integrated structure and apply their checks accordingly.

Update checks to operate on the strategy hypothesis fields (e.g., stop-loss, target, urgency) and canonical state rather than on microstructure features computed elsewhere.

Return veto reasons in a consistent way for logging and telemetry. This will help the router and strategy registry summarise why trades were vetoed.



9. CA_Trading_System_EA/include/Execution.mqh


Current:
Sends orders to the trading platform. It uses the selected candidate’s information to create market or limit orders, manage positions, etc.


Required changes:


Accept the FinalStrategyIntegratedStateVector_t and extract entry_price, stop_loss, take_profit, direction, urgent flag, etc., from the StrategyHypothesis_t inside.

Use the strategy’s execution_style (e.g., market vs limit, reduce‑only vs full size) to choose the order type. This may be part of the StrategyHypothesis_t.

Keep existing position management logic largely intact, but ensure all values (e.g., risk per trade) come from the risk engine output and not from ad‑hoc computations.



10. CA_Trading_System_EA/include/State.mqh and Config.mqh


Required changes:


Extend state structures to include the new transport objects (e.g., RawSignalBank_t raw_bank; CategorySelectedVector_t cat_selected; StrategyHypothesisBank_t hypotheses; FinalStrategyIntegratedStateVector_t final_vector;). Ensure they are initialised and reset appropriately.

Add configuration parameters for new gating thresholds (SignalStack gate threshold, LocationPass threshold, Category confirmation counts, dynamic vs fixed selection, etc.).

Expose settings for strategies to specify their required category confirmations, structural masks, risk templates, etc. These should be part of the user inputs at the top of the EA.



11. CA_Trading_System_EA/include/MarketScannerHub.mqh and Confluence.mqh


Required changes:


Ensure scanning outputs are passed only to the confluence engine and not directly used by strategies. The raw scanner output should feed into Confluence then into InstitutionalStateVector.

Confluence engine should produce aggregated signals consistent with canonical raw state assembly. Avoid computing signals outside the InstitutionalStateVector. The confluence should thus be integrated into the raw signal bank builder.



12. Strategy files (e.g., Strat_MainTradingLogic.mqh, Strat_Trend_VWAPPullback.mqh, Strat_ICT_SilverBullet.mqh, etc.)


Required changes:


Adopt the new IStrategy interface. Each strategy file must:
Provide a unique Id().

Implement ShouldRun() using canonical state and category passes.

Construct and return a StrategyHypothesis_t inside Evaluate().

Replace any internal computations of microstructure, order flow, or category selection with calls to the canonical state (e.g., raw_bank.ofi_z, cat_selected.trend_signal_id).

Define strategy‑specific parameters (regime, required category confirmations, required structure masks, invalidation models) as constants or configurable inputs and apply them in gating.

Return veto reasons in the hypothesis when gating fails at a later stage. For example, if LocationGate() fails, set invalidation_reason = "location_fail".

Remove direct order placement from strategies; strategy files should only emit hypotheses.



13. Other supporting files


CA_Trading_System_EA/include/PositionMgmt.mqh:
Should operate on integrated state, not on raw candidates. Update functions to read from FinalStrategyIntegratedStateVector_t.

CA_Trading_System_EA/include/Types.mqh and Enums:
Define new enumerations and structs for StrategyHypothesis_t, RawSignalBank_t, CategorySelectedVector_t, SignalStackGate_t, LocationPass_t, etc.

Deprecate or remove redundant candidate structures that are tied to the old architecture.

CA_Trading_System_EA/include/BuildFlags.mqh and compile‑time macros:
Add flags for enabling/disabling the new architecture or fallback to legacy mode. This might ease migration.



14. Summary of Flow After Changes


1. MarketScannerHub collects snapshots/scans.
2. Confluence engine merges and scores signals.
3. InstitutionalStateVector builds RawSignalBank_t and canonical market state (includes degrade logic, coverage, context).
4. CategorySelector selects one signal per category, produces CategorySelectedVector_t, CategoryPassVector_t, SignalStackGate_t, LocationPass_t, and context vectors.
5. StrategyHypothesisBank is cleared.
6. StrategyRegistry iterates through registered strategies, runs gating via ShouldRun(), then Evaluate() to produce StrategyHypothesis_t.  Each hypothesis is added to the bank.
7. Router receives StrategyHypothesisBank, integrates with canonical state to produce FinalStrategyIntegratedStateVector_t for each hypothesis, scores them, selects the top one, and sends it to Policies/RiskEngine.
8. Policies and RiskEngine veto or adjust as necessary.
9. Execution module places the order for the final trade decision.



Final notes


Testing and migration: The proposed changes affect nearly every layer of the EA. Introduce compile‑time flags to enable the new pipeline gradually, maintain backward compatibility where possible, and build unit tests for each stage (InstitutionalStateVector, CategorySelector, StrategyHypothesis generation, routing, and risk).

Documentation: Document each new type and function thoroughly. Provide diagrams of the new data flow so that other developers understand the separation of responsibilities between canonical state, category selection, strategy interpretation, routing, and risk/execution.

Performance considerations: Because the RawSignalBank_t is large, pass it by reference whenever possible. Similarly, sorting or copying large numbers of hypotheses should be minimised. Monitor CPU usage on OnTimer and OnTick events to ensure the EA remains performant.


Implementing this per‑file plan will restructure the CA Trading System EA to follow the recommended architecture: signals → canonical state → category selection → strategy hypotheses → final integrated vector → router → risk/policies → execution. This separation of concerns will enable single‑ownership of market truth, prevent duplicate computations, and allow strategies to focus on interpreting the canonical state rather than building it.
